findings.message: DeleteButton performs an irreversible destructive action with no confirmation step. A misclick immediately deletes the item with no way to recover.
findings.suggestion: Add a confirmation dialog (e.g., window.confirm('Are you sure you want to delete this item?') or a modal) before issuing the DELETE request.

findings.message: No disabled-while-pending state: the button remains clickable while the DELETE fetch is in flight, allowing duplicate requests and giving no feedback that the action is processing.
findings.suggestion: Track a loading state (e.g., useState<boolean>) and set the button to disabled with a loading label while the request is pending.

findings.message: No error feedback: the DELETE fetch response is not checked for failure (non-2xx status or network error). If the delete fails, the page reloads silently and the user has no idea the action did not succeed.
findings.suggestion: Check the response status and catch errors; show an error message (e.g., set an error state rendered as a visible alert) before reloading or instead of reloading on failure.
